zh.lifeSummary: 當Samuel Lemuel Drew於1799年出生在美國緬因州約克利明頓時，他的父親Lemuel Drew30歲，母親Nancy Chadwell27歲。他於1820年11月21日與Olive Abigail Randall在美國緬因州約克貝維其可結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1850年住在美國緬因州阿盧斯圖可斯米納且於1870年住在美國緬因州阿盧斯圖可。他去世於1877年6月6日在美國緬因州阿盧斯圖可代爾 布魯克享年78歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Samuel Lemuel Drew于1799年出生在美国缅因州約克利明頓时，他的父亲Lemuel Drew30岁，母亲Nancy Chadwell27岁。他于1820年11月21日与Olive Abigail Randall在美国缅因州約克貝維其可结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1850年住在美国缅因州阿盧斯圖可斯米納且于1870年住在美国缅因州阿盧斯圖可。他去世于1877年6月6日在美国缅因州阿盧斯圖可代爾 布魯克享年78岁。

nameMeanings.name: Drew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from a Norman French personal name Dreus based on ancient Germanic Drogo, the name of one of the sons of Charlemagne (compare Gothic driugan ‘to serve as a soldier’). In Old French this name became Dreus, Drues (nominative) and Dru, Driu, Dreu (accusative) and was introduced to England by the Normans. For further information about this name, see also Montague .
English: from the personal name Drew, a pet form of Andrew which was confused with the same name of Old French origin (see 1 above).

Irish: when not an adoption of the English surname, a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Druaidh or Ó Druaidh or Ó Draoi ‘son’ and ‘descendant of the druid’, from draoi ‘druid’, genitive druadh or draoi. All of these were often Anglicized as Drury .

variants.name: Drews
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Gerhard, Gunter, Hans, Heinz, Helmut, Otto, Reinhold, Bernd, Detlef, Egon, Erna.
English: variant of Drew with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is also found in Guernsey in the Channel Islands.
Americanized form of German, Danish, and Dutch   Drewes  .

variants.name: Drewer
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Droart, Drouart (which is probably from ancient Germanic Droghard, with the first element as in Drew + hard ‘brave, hardy’).

variants.name: Drewett
meanings: English (Wiltshire, Berkshire, and Surrey): of Norman origin, from the Old French personal name Druet, a diminutive of Drue, Dreu (from ancient Germanic Drogo); see Drew 1. Alternatively, the name may be from a diminutive of Old French dru ‘lover’; see Drew 4.

variants.name: Dronet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcee, Desire, Olympe.

French (mainly Loire-Atlantique): from a diminutive of the personal name Dron, derived from the ancient Germanic name Drogo, a short form of compound names based on the element drog, presumably meaning ‘battle’ (see also Drew ).

variants.name: Droz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Aida, Cruz Maria, Diego, Jose, Miguel, Milagros, Nydia, Osvaldo, Rodriguez, Vidal.

French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Drogo, a short form of compound names based on the element drog, presumably meaning ‘battle’ (see also Drew ). This surname is also found in Puerto Rico.

variants.name: Drea
meanings: Irish (Kilkenny): shortened Anglicized form of Ó Draoi (see Drew ).

variants.name: Montague
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from either Montaigu-le-bois (Manche) or Montaigu (Manche, Aisne), named with Old French mont ‘hill’ + agu ‘pointed’ (from Latin acutus). Drogo de Montagud was a companion of William the Conqueror and founder of the Montagu(e) family, among whom the personal name Drogo was revived in the 19th century. Another family of the name Monatgu have held the titles of Duke of Montagu, Earl of Sandwich, Earl and Duke of Manchester, Earl of Halifax, and Earl of Beaulieu, and.
Irish: adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Mac Taidhg (see McTigue ).

variants.name: Drouin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Armand, Benoit, Jacques, Andre, Clermont, Emile, Gaston, Gilles, Ovide, Pierre, Serge.

French: from a pet form of the Old French personal name Droue, a variant of Dreue, which is from the ancient Germanic name Drogo, a short form of compound names based on the element drog, presumably meaning ‘battle’ (see also Drew ). Compare Derouen , Derouin , and Tron .

variants.name: Drury
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Drury  .

variants.name: Drouhard
meanings: French:
from the ancient Germanic personal name Droghard, composed of the elements drog (presumably meaning ‘battle’; see also Drew ) and hard ‘hard, strong’.
alternatively, a derivative of the ancient Germanic name Drogo, a short form of compound names like Droghard (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Samuel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name (Hebrew Shemuel), possibly meaning ‘He (God) has hearkened’ (presumably to the prayers of a mother for a son). It may also be understood as a contracted form of Hebrew sha'ulme'el meaning ‘asked of God’. In the case of Samuel the son of Hannah, this would be more in keeping with his mother's statement ‘Because I have asked him of the Lord’ (1 Samuel 1:20). Living in the 11th century bc , Samuel was a Hebrew judge and prophet of the greatest historical importance, who established the Hebrew monarchy, anointing as king both Saul and, later, David. In the Authorized Version two books of the Old Testament are named after him, although in Roman Catholic and Orthodox versions of the Bible they are known as the first and second Book of Kings. The story of Samuel being called by God while still a child serving in the house of Eli the priest (1 Samuel 3) is of great vividness and has moved countless generations. In England and America the name was particularly popular among the 16th-century Puritans and among Nonconformists from the 17th to the 19th century. It became fashionable again in the 1990s.

nameMeanings.name: Lemuel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Possibly meaning ‘devoted to God’ in Hebrew; it was borne in the Bible by an obscure king who was lectured by his mother on the perils of strong drink and the virtues of a dutiful wife (Proverbs 31). He is mentioned by  Chaucer  in The Canterbury Tales, where his name is carefully distinguished from the more familiar   Samuel .  Lemuel Gulliver was the unusual name of the hero of  Jonathan Swift's  Gulliver's Travels (  1726 ).

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.

historicEvents.title: Whitehead Light
historicEvents.description: In 1804, President Thomas Jefferson authorized the creation of a light station on Whitehead Island. The light house went into service by 1807. It is the third-oldest light house in Maine. Whitehead Light still exists as the private property of Pine Island Camp, a non-profit organization.

historicEvents.title: Making States Equal
historicEvents.description: The Missouri Compromise helped provide the entrance of Maine as a free state and Missouri as a slave state into the United States. As part of the compromise, slavery was prohibited north of the 36°30′ parallel, excluding Missouri.
Historical Boundaries: 1839: Aroostock, Maine, United States
Historical Boundaries: 1844: Aroostook, Maine, United States
U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.
Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

historicEvents.title: The Battle at Gettysburg
historicEvents.description: The Battle of Gettysburg involved the largest number of casualties of the entire Civil war and is often described as the war's turning point. Between 46,000 and 51,000 soldiers lost their lives during the three-day Battle. To honor the fallen soldiers, President Abraham Lincoln read his historic Gettysburg Address and helped those listening by redefining the purpose of the war.

en.lifeSummary: When Samuel Lemuel Drew was born in 1799, in Limington, York, Maine, United States, his father, Lemuel Drew, was 30 and his mother, Nancy Chadwell, was 27. He married Olive Abigail Randall on 21 November 1820, in Berwick, York, Maine, United States. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He lived in Smyrna, Aroostook, Maine, United States in 1850 and Aroostook, Maine, United States in 1870. He died on 6 June 1877, in Dyer Brook, Aroostook, Maine, United States, at the age of 78.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Samuel Lemuel Drew est né en 1799, à Limington, York, Maine, États-Unis, son père, Lemuel Drew, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Nancy Chadwell, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Olive Abigail Randall le 21 novembre 1820, à Berwick, York, Maine, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il a vécu à Smyrna, Aroostook, Maine, États-Unis en 1850 et à Aroostook, Maine, États-Unis en 1870. Il est décédé le 6 juin 1877, à Dyer Brook, Aroostook, Maine, États-Unis, à l’âge de 78 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Samuel Lemuel Drew 1799 in Limington, York, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lemuel Drew 30 und seine Mutter Nancy Chadwell 27. Er hat am 21. November 1820 in Berwick, York, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten Olive Abigail Randall geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. 1850 hat er in Smyrna, Aroostook, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten und 1870 in Aroostook, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Er starb am 6. Juni 1877 im Alter von 78 Jahren in Dyer Brook, Aroostook, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Samuel Lemuel Drew è nato il nel 1799 nel Limington, York, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America, il suo padre, Lemuel Drew, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Nancy Chadwell, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Olive Abigail Randall il 21 novembre 1820, nel Berwick, York, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Vissuto a Smyrna, Aroostook, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1850 e nel Aroostook, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1870. Morì il 6 giugno 1877, nel Dyer Brook, Aroostook, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 78 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Samuel Lemuel Drewが1799年にアメリカ合衆国メイン州ヨークリミントンで生まれた時，父Lemuel Drewは30歳，母Nancy Chadwellは27歳であった。1820年11月21日にアメリカ合衆国メイン州ヨークバーウィックでOlive Abigail Randallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1850年にはアメリカ合衆国メイン州アルーストオクスムイルナに，1870年にはアメリカ合衆国メイン州アルーストオクに住んでいた。彼は1877年6月6日にアメリカ合衆国メイン州アルーストオクディエル ブルークで78歳で死去した。

ko.New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine: 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 뉴 라임릭

ko.lifeSummary: Samuel Lemuel Drew(이)가 1799년 미국 메인 주 요르크 림잉턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lemuel Drew(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Nancy Chadwell(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1820년 11월 21일 미국 메인 주 요르크 버윅에서 Olive Abigail Randall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1850년도에 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 스미른아에서, 1870년도에 미국 메인 주 아루스투크에서 살았다. 그는 1877년 6월 6일 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 다예르 브룩에서 78세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Samuel Lemuel Drew nasceu em 1799, em Limington, York, Maine, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Lemuel Drew, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Nancy Chadwell, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Olive Abigail Randall em 21 de novembro de 1820, em Berwick, York, Maine, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele viveu em Smyrna, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos da América em 1850 e em Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos da América em 1870. Ele faleceu em 6 de junho de 1877, em Dyer Brook, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos da América, com 78 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Samuel Lemuel Drew родился в 1799 г. в местности лимингтон, ерк, Мэн, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Lemuel Drew было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Nancy Chadwell, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Olive Abigail Randall 21 ноября 1820 г. в местности бирвикк, ерк, Мэн, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он жил в местности смирна, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты в 1850 году и в местности арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты в 1870 году. Он умер 6 июня 1877 г. в местности диир брук, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 78 лет.

Se casó con Olive Abigail Randall el 21 de noviembre de 1820, en Berwick, York, Maine, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Vivió en Smyrna, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos en 1850 y en Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos en 1870.
